subject: LU-16843 ldiskfs: extend journal before merging extent blocks

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

hmm, but problem was hit not with regular journal credits rather with revoke records?

Patch Set 1:

Builds for x86_64 centos7,rhel8.5 successful
Job output URL: https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/37967/results.html

 Commencing initial testing: runtests@ldiskfs+DNE runtests@ldiskfs runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> hmm, but problem was hit not with regular journal credits rather with revoke records?

revoke credits was not introduced in the earlier kernel version, the rhel8.7 version of patch is created with revoke credits in place.
IMHO I still think when potentially removing the extent blocks along the path, we should make sure there are enough journal credits to cover the block bitmap and group desc block for each extent block.

Patch Set 1:

I guess we should rather use:
#define ldiskfs_journal_start_with_revoke(inode, type, blocks, revoke_creds) \
	__ldiskfs_journal_start((inode), __LINE__, (type), (blocks), 0,	\
			     (revoke_creds))
when a big IO is declared

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> revoke credits was not introduced in the earlier kernel version, the rhel8.7 version of patch is created with revoke credits in place.

the problem reported by Ihara is exactly about revoke records.

> IMHO I still think when potentially removing the extent blocks along the path, we should make sure there are enough journal credits to cover the block bitmap and group desc block for each extent block.

we already calculate records enough to grow tree and insert full chain of blocks, which is essentially reverse operation.

Patch Set 1:

Initial testing succeeded.

Succeeded:
- runtests@ldiskfs+DNE runtests@ldiskfs runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey

parents.subject: LU-17046 tests: fix write success in 1g

commit.subject: LU-16843 ldiskfs: extend journal before merging extent blocks
commit.message:
LU-16843 ldiskfs: extend journal before merging extent blocks

Make sure we have enough journal credits when trying
to merge extent blocks, especially the revoke credits.

Change-Id: I53f05da39c4e910b8a1e1936b910469034c51e44
Fixes: 0f7e6c02a9 ("LU-16843 ldiskfs: merge extent blocks")
Signed-off-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
